title: Awesome AGI and ASI
description: The most comprehensive, curated collection of resources on Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) and Artificial Superintelligence (ASI) -- covering frameworks, agents, LLMs, RAG, fine-tuning, safety, alignment, research papers, and tools for builders and researchers shaping the future of intelligence.

items.title: Announcement
items.content_html: Announcement

items.title: Compute, Hardware & Quantization
items.content_html: AI compute infrastructure, custom silicon, quantization tools, neuromorphic chips, and decentralized compute networks powering the path to AGI and ASI.

items.title: AGI Benchmarks & Evals
items.content_html: Post-Turing AGI benchmarks and evaluation frameworks tracking the path from narrow AI to general intelligence -- ARC-AGI, SWE-bench, GAIA, GPQA, and more.

items.title: DevOps Guide
items.content_html: Developer and DevOps guide for the Awesome AGI and ASI site -- GitHub Actions CI/CD, MkDocs Material configuration, SEO setup, and local development.

items.title: Home
items.content_html: The most comprehensive curated collection of AGI and ASI resources -- frameworks, agents, LLMs, RAG, fine-tuning, safety, alignment, research papers, and tools for AI builders.

items.title: Contributing
items.content_html: Guidelines for contributing resources to the Awesome AGI and ASI repository, including formatting standards and submission process.

items.title: Blog
items.content_html: Updates, deep dives, and commentary on the journey from AI to AGI to ASI -- the Awesome AGI and ASI blog.

items.title: Welcome to Awesome AGI and ASI
items.content_html:
Welcome to Awesome AGI and ASI

The most comprehensive, curated collection of resources on the journey from AI to AGI to ASI is now available as a website with full-text search, clean navigation, and a minimal reading experience.

items.title: Agents
items.content_html: Autonomous AI systems that perceive, reason, and act in the world. These agents represent the closest things we have to AGI-like behavior in narrow domains -- writing code, conducting research, operating computers, and navigating virtual worlds.

items.title: Frameworks and Platforms
items.content_html: The infrastructure powering the next generation of autonomous AI. These frameworks let you build, orchestrate, and deploy AI agents that can reason, browse, code, and collaborate -- from single-agent tools to multi-agent orchestration platforms.

items.title: Paper-to-Code Automation
items.content_html: The leap from research paper to working implementation is one of the biggest bottlenecks in AI progress. These tools automate the translation of academic papers into production-ready code repositories -- accelerating the research-to-engineering pipeline and democratizing access to frontier techniques.

items.title: Physical AI & Embodied Intelligence
items.content_html: AGI cannot exist only in text -- it must understand and act in the physical world. **Physical AI** bridges foundation models with embodiment: humanoid robots, manipulation, navigation, and physics simulation. Moravec's Paradox -- sensorimotor skills are harder than abstract reasoning -- remains one of the deepest unsolved AGI challenges.

items.title: Data Infrastructure for AI at Scale
items.content_html: AGI systems are fundamentally bound by their data layers. Training frontier models requires petabyte-scale data pipelines, and deploying them requires infrastructure that can serve millions of requests with sub-second latency. The SRE and data engineering challenges behind superintelligence are as hard as the ML itself.

items.title: LLM Deployment and Serving
items.content_html: Getting models from research to production. High-performance inference engines, serving frameworks, and optimization tools that make LLMs fast, cost-efficient, and scalable.

items.title: Distributed Training Frameworks
items.content_html: Training frontier models requires distributing computation across thousands of GPUs. These frameworks handle the parallelism, memory optimization, and communication needed to train models with billions (or trillions) of parameters.

items.title: LLM Fine-Tuning Techniques
items.content_html: Making foundation models your own. Fine-tuning adapts pre-trained LLMs to specific tasks, domains, or behaviors -- from lightweight LoRA adapters that train in hours on a single GPU to full alignment techniques like DPO that shape model values.

items.title: LLM Application Frameworks
items.content_html: The developer toolkits for building production LLM applications. From orchestration chains to observability platforms, these frameworks are the backbone of every AI product shipping today.

items.title: Prompt Engineering
items.content_html: The art and science of communicating with LLMs. These techniques transform how models reason, from simple chain-of-thought to sophisticated graph-structured exploration of solution spaces.

items.title: RAG and Vector Databases
items.content_html: RAG grounds LLMs in real-world data, eliminating hallucinations and enabling knowledge-intensive applications. Full RAG stack: vector storage, retrieval engines, graph RAG, document parsing, and embedding models.

items.title: Top Conferences
items.content_html: Where the AGI community gathers. From peer-reviewed academic venues publishing foundational research to government-level safety summits and industry developer conferences shaping the future of AI.

items.title: Papers, Blogs, Courses and Lectures
items.content_html: The research frontier -- cutting-edge papers on capabilities, reasoning, agents, alignment, and interpretability defining the path from LLMs to AGI.

items.title: Tutorials and Guides
items.content_html: Hands-on learning resources to go from understanding AI concepts to building production systems. Courses, documentation, and curated collections for every skill level.

items.title: Safety, Alignment and Governance
items.content_html: The most critical section. Ensuring AI remains safe, aligned with human values, and under meaningful control is the defining challenge as systems grow more capable.

items.title: Understanding AI, AGI, and ASI
items.content_html: Learn the differences between Artificial Intelligence, Artificial General Intelligence, and Superintelligence -- capabilities, comparison table, DeepMind AGI levels, and future implications.

items.title: ASI and Superintelligence Research
items.content_html: The long game. This section tracks the organizations racing toward AGI/ASI, the books that frame the debate, the seminal papers that define the field, the benchmarks that measure progress, and the roadmaps that predict when -- and how -- we get there.
